quotes: Test contrast levels (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair. [GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]
Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Ensure text accessibility and compliance with WCAG AA contrast standards across all designs.
rules.rule_text: All background and text color pairs must pass WCAG AA contrast ratios (minimum 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text >=24px or 19px bold). Use known-safe pairs such as Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003D74), Dark Navy (#011E45), or Graphite (#212121) on White (#ffffff), Light Blue (#E6F0F9), or Golden Sand (#FEF1C8). Never use Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) text on a White (#ffffff) background.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Support visually impaired or colorblind users by ensuring information is accessible through multiple channels.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning or hierarchical relationships. Always pair color treatments with icons, visual depth, explicit copy, or formatting cues.
